sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an agreement and any
amendments with the W-SW WBLS West Owner IX, L.P., (“Developer”) for payment for
all improvements mitigating the impact of their Development (Project) at the Bancroft
Way at-grade railroad crossing.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Developer is responsible for all actual costs and expenses for the Union Pacific
Railroad’s (UPRR) performance of the safety improvement work at Bancroft Way
crossing pertaining to their Project. One main purpose of this agreement is to document
the process pursuant to which Developer shall assume responsibility for all such
railroad costs.
The agreement further confirms that Developer and its contractor(s) are responsible for
complying with all UPRR insurance and other requirements applicable to any work
performed by the Developer with respect to Developer’s portion of the Project Railroad
Improvements.
Developer and City agree that Developer shall be responsible for making all payments
to the UPRR. There is no fiscal impact to the City as a result of this agreement.
sections.current_situation:
Within Project area there is an at-grade railroad crossing at Bancroft Way. The
projected impacts of the Developer. The Development will require improvements to
safely facilitate all road users through this intersection.
The City, Developer and UPRR have agreed on the Scope of Work for Developer’s
Work Associated with the Railroad Crossing at Bancroft Way, shown in Schedule 1
within Attachment 2.

sections.background:
The Developer is constructing a new Project at 747 Bancroft Way, which consists of a
159,143-square-foot, three-story building containing 124,539 square feet of research
and development space and 34,604 square feet of light manufacturing space, and a
surface parking lot containing 76 off-street parking spaces and five loading spaces.
Pursuant to Use Permit # ZP2021-0096, the Project is subject to certain conditions of
approval that require Developer to coordinate with the UPRR, the California Public
Utilities Commission (CPUC) and the City to improve the safety of all travelers at the
Bancroft Way at-grade railroad crossing next to Project site.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Ensuring that the developer works with UPRR to make the Bancroft Way railroad
crossing safe is critical to supporting biking and walking in Berkeley. Biking and walking
are the most environmentally sustainable modes of transportation, and the City’s
Climate Action Plan seeks to make them the “primary means of transportation for
Berkeley residents and visitors.” Bancroft Way is a key connection in the biking and
walking network, bringing people of all ages and abilities to and from Aquatic Park and
the pedestrian and bicyclist bridge over I-80 to the waterfront. What’s more, this
development, and others nearby, will increase bike traffic, making it even more
important to ensure safety for vulnerable road users.
sections.rationale:
The UPRR does not enter into agreements with private entities. Therefore, the City will
enter into an agreement with the UPRR on behalf of the Developer with the Developer
is agreeing to pay all the associated UPRR costs.
sections.alternatives:
Since Union Pacific does not enter into agreements with private entities, the City is
acting as the go-between between Developer and Union Pacific in order for the safety
improvements to be implemented. If the City does not enter into this agreement, UPRR
will not approve the project, therefore no alternatives are feasible.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, (510) 981-6396
Hamid Mostowfi, Transportation Division Manager, (510) 981-6403

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
AGREEMENT: REIMBURSEMENT AGREEMENT WITH STEELWAVE
DEVELOPMENT FOR STEELWAVE’S CONSTRUCTION OF PUBLIC FACILITIES
WHEREAS, W-SW WBLS West Owner IX, L.P. is engaged in the development of certain
real property located at 747 Bancroft Way in the City; and
WHEREAS, Within the development project area there is an at-grade railroad crossing at
Bancroft; and
WHEREAS, The impacts of the W-SW WBLS West Owner IX, L.P. will require
improvements to safely facilitate all road users through this intersection; and
WHEREAS, Development of the project under the use permit requires construction of
certain traffic improvements, which consist of the portion of Union Pacific Railroad
crossings safety improvements pertaining to the SteelWave project at Bancroft Way
crossing, collectively "Public Facilities" in the project area that will serve W-SW WBLS
West Owner IX, L.P. as well as the public; and
WHEREAS, Since Union Pacific does not enter into agreements with private entities the
City will act as the go-between between Developer and Union Pacific in order for the
safety improvements to be implemented; and
WHEREAS, Developer acknowledges responsibility and will pay for all actual costs and
expenses for the UPRR parties performance of the safety improvement work pertaining
to Developer’s portion of the railroad improvements at the railroad crossing; and
WHEREAS, City of Berkeley will submit invoices for implementation of “Public Facilities”
by Union Pacific to W-SW WBLS West Owner IX, L.P. for payment directly to Union
Pacific.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that a
reimbursement agreement and any amendments with W-SW WBLS West Owner IX, L.P.
for payment of implementation of Developer’s portion of safety improvements by Union
Pacific at rai
